self_format_family: .raw — universal rules SSOT (raw 0 root-ssot triad, raw 2 self-format grammar)
.own — project-local rules (parallel namespace to .raw, same grammar)

violation_inventory_2026-04-27.violation_axis: note count > 2 — primary; cumulative note chars + verbatim quote secondary
violation_inventory_2026-04-27.drift_root_cause: strengthening cycles (2026-04-27 patches), incident evidence (kick-infra-fallback), deferred follow-up rationale, cross-rule relation prose all accumulate as `note` lines because no other structured key fits without raw 2 keys-whitelist extension
violation_inventory_2026-04-27.cross_repo_propagation_risk: sister repos (nexus / anima / hexa-lang / airgenome / n6-architecture / hive) inherit raw 47 strategy-exploration-omega-cycle and raw 0 root-ssot — same drift expected within 30-90d once they bootstrap their own .raw contents

five_falsifier_pre_register_raw_71.condition: 30d post-implementation: ssot-minimal-lint-violator count remains > 5 (no improvement vs 2026-04-27 baseline of 7)
five_falsifier_pre_register_raw_71.experiment_or_observation: hexa tool/ssot_minimal_lint.hexa scan --strict | grep VIOLATION | wc -l, weekly
five_falsifier_pre_register_raw_71.mechanism: If chosen separation design (option a/b/c/d/e/f/g/h/i/j) does not actually reduce note count, then design is decorative not effective

five_falsifier_pre_register_raw_71.condition: 3+ legitimate ω-cycle strengthening events cannot be expressed in the chosen separation channel without information loss
five_falsifier_pre_register_raw_71.experiment_or_observation: review 90d strengthening commits; manually classify; count those that needed inline note despite separation channel availability
five_falsifier_pre_register_raw_71.mechanism: If separation grammar is too narrow for legitimate uses (e.g., date-pinned multi-paragraph history), retire and broaden

five_falsifier_pre_register_raw_71.experiment_or_observation: git rm <new-ssot-file>; run tool/root_ssot_check.hexa + tool/guide_lint.hexa + raw_cli.hexa verify; expect PASS (project still recoverable from triad alone)
five_falsifier_pre_register_raw_71.mechanism: Triad invariant requires that .raw + .own + .guide alone reconstruct project semantics. If chosen design promotes a NEW root SSOT, F3 fires.

five_falsifier_pre_register_raw_71.mechanism: raw 47 cross-repo mandate fails if <3 sister repos adopt; design has poor portability

axis_2_grammar_compatibility.name: raw 2 self-format grammar compatibility
axis_2_grammar_compatibility.rationale_for_orthogonality: axis 1 = semantic root invariant, axis 2 = byte-level grammar; correlation < 0.7 because option b (new root file with old grammar) decouples the two

axis_4_audit_trail_completeness.name: strengthening-history retrievability
axis_4_audit_trail_completeness.source: manual classification of N=10 sample strengthenings

ten_options_explored_full_ladder.name: .annex.<rule-id> separate file
ten_options_explored_full_ladder.design: one annex file per rule that exceeds note budget. e.g., .annex.raw-100, .annex.raw-108. Same raw 2 grammar (header + body keys). Linked from main rule via single 'annex' key.
pros: 1-rule-1-annex isolates blast radius
raw 2 grammar reusable as-is
natural per-rule audit boundary
cons: proliferation: 7 violators today, possibly 20+ at scale = 20+ files in repo root
discovery cost: cold-entry agent must scan annex.* glob
ten_options_explored_full_ladder.cost: MID — 1 lint extension + 1 grammar key + glob discovery in cold-entry

ten_options_explored_full_ladder.name: .note ledger root SSOT (quad-extension)
ten_options_explored_full_ladder.design: single root file .note adjacent to .raw .own .guide. Stores all multi-note overflow + strengthening history globally. Each entry: rule-id-ref + ts + kind {strengthening|incident|history|evidence|deferral} + body. raw 0 triad becomes quad: .raw + .own + .guide + .note.
pros: single discovery surface (cold-entry agent reads 4 files instead of 4 + glob)
natural cross-rule audit (timeline)
raw 77 audit-ledger pattern reuse
cons: raw 0 triad invariant breaks (F3 risk) — quad-extension not invariant-preserving without explicit raw 0 STRENGTHEN
single-point write contention if many rules strengthen same day
ten_options_explored_full_ladder.cost: HIGH — requires raw 0 STRENGTHEN (triad → quad); 1 new root file; lint extension for cross-ref validation

ten_options_explored_full_ladder.name: inline structured-keys (strengthening / history / evidence)
ten_options_explored_full_ladder.design: extend raw 2 keys whitelist with `strengthening` (date + body), `history` (date + change), `evidence` (path/sha + claim), `incident` (ts + cause + remediation). Rule body keeps these as separate structured keys instead of `note`. note count cap stays at 2 for true free-form prose.
pros: zero new files — pure grammar extension
raw 2 self-format grammar absorbs cleanly (precedent: raw 47 added strategy-source / cross-repo-trawl-witness)
ssot_minimal_lint cap on `note` becomes accurate (note = unstructured prose)
naturally machine-parseable per axis
cons: body line cap (60) still applies — pushes line-budget pressure rather than eliminating it
key explosion in raw 2 keys whitelist (already large)
history kind variety (strengthening / incident / deferral / evidence) becomes 4+ new keys
ten_options_explored_full_ladder.cost: LOW — keys whitelist append + lint counter recategorization

ten_options_explored_full_ladder.name: .history append-only chain (raw 77 audit-ledger pattern)
ten_options_explored_full_ladder.design: per-repo .history file (raw 77 audit-ledger schema). JSONL-equivalent in raw 2 grammar — append-only, never edited. Each row: ts + rule-ref + change-kind + body-hash + classifier-version. note overflow goes to .history rows; rule body keeps abstract pointer.
pros: raw 77 audit-ledger pattern already accepted across repos
append-only = perfect audit trail
rule body stays minimal — pointer only
cons: history retrieval requires scan + filter (no random access)
JSONL-as-raw-2-grammar requires ordered keys (raw 2 supports same-key-many-lines so OK)
two artifacts to keep in sync (.raw rule pointer vs .history row)

raw_compat.1: lock applies (uappnd not uchg for append-only)

ten_options_explored_full_ladder.cost: MID — 1 new root file + raw 77 schema reuse + uappnd lock variant

ten_options_explored_full_ladder.name: annex-as-attribute (short ref + external doc)
ten_options_explored_full_ladder.design: rule keeps short summary in body; long history lives in docs/<slug>.md (markdown with ai-native frontmatter). rule body has 'annex-doc' key pointing to docs path. ssot_minimal_lint scope unchanged (markdown out of scope per raw 101 Tier-B).
pros: zero new SSOT format — uses existing docs/*.md
natural for human-readable strengthening narratives
low cost
cons: docs/*.md NOT machine-parseable to raw 2 grammar (Tier-B not Tier-A)
split semantic — agent must follow pointer to fully audit a rule
raw 91 C4 citation discipline easier to violate (docs drift)
ten_options_explored_full_ladder.cost: LOW — pointer key + existing docs convention

ten_options_explored_full_ladder.name: date-pinned note paragraphs (latest-first grouping)
ten_options_explored_full_ladder.design: keep note in rule body BUT structure as `note <ts> <body>` with latest-first ordering. ssot_minimal_lint relaxes 2-note cap to N-note where N = unique-date count, enforce per-date cap of 1 note. Effective: rule body can hold 1 strengthening per day, all dates collected.
pros: minimal grammar change — note becomes ts-prefixed
natural latest-first navigation
cons: body line cap (60) still binding — can't grow indefinitely
lint relaxation weakens raw 101 minimal claim
violates raw 101 spec's '2 note cap' falsifier — would require raw 101 STRENGTHEN
ten_options_explored_full_ladder.cost: LOW grammar / HIGH governance — needs raw 101 lint threshold change

ten_options_explored_full_ladder.name: per-rule.md ai-native frontmatter
ten_options_explored_full_ladder.design: each rule lives in its own docs/raws/<slug>.md with YAML-like frontmatter (ai-native, NOT JSON) and markdown body. .raw becomes index file pointing to per-rule.md.
pros: maximum body real estate per rule — no inline cap
human + machine readable
cons: BREAKS raw 0 single-file root SSOT — explodes to 116+ files
BREAKS raw 1 single-chflags-uchg — must lock 116+ files individually
BREAKS raw 2 self-format grammar (yaml frontmatter ≠ raw 2)
DESIGN INVERSION — opposite of consolidation
ten_options_explored_full_ladder.cost: VERY HIGH — invalidates 4+ rules; not viable
ten_options_explored_full_ladder.verdict: REJECT — counter-example for the design (lists why option g is NOT applicable)

ten_options_explored_full_ladder.name: hierarchical raw (parent + child sub-rules)
ten_options_explored_full_ladder.design: raw 100 becomes parent + raw 100.1, 100.2 children for strengthening blocks. raw 2 grammar gains nested numbering.
pros: natural strengthening tree
rule-id stability (parent unchanged)
cons: raw 2 numbering grammar drift (decimal numbers)
raw 87 paired-roadmap-id mapping ambiguous (which child maps?)
ssot_minimal_lint per-rule body cap re-applies to each child — same line-budget pressure
ten_options_explored_full_ladder.cost: MID — grammar extension + raw 87 mapping rule

ten_options_explored_full_ladder.name: dual-tier raw (core 60-line invariant + extended annex non-required)
ten_options_explored_full_ladder.design: split each rule into CORE (mandatory, ssot_minimal_lint enforced) + EXTENDED (optional, separate file or section, no cap). CORE = invariant declaration; EXTENDED = strengthening / history / incident.
pros: preserves raw 101 minimal claim on CORE
EXTENDED has no cap — strengthening freedom
matches mathematical 'spec-vs-implementation' decoupling
cons: two files OR two-section parser — non-trivial
subset of option a (annex per rule) but with explicit core/extended boundary
ten_options_explored_full_ladder.cost: MID — section grammar OR annex glob + lint scoping

ten_options_explored_full_ladder.name: note → @evidence chain (raw 71 falsifier-style binding)
ten_options_explored_full_ladder.design: convert each note into one of: (a) `falsifier` row (raw 71 schema), (b) `evidence` row (raw 91 C4 citation), (c) `proof` row (already in raw 2). note becomes residual unstructured prose only.
pros: all overflow becomes structured (axes 3 ratio improves)
raw 71 + raw 91 + raw 2 keys already exist — no new keys
perfectly aligned with raw 101 spec spirit (no redundant prose)
cons: semantic remapping cost (each existing note must classify into falsifier vs evidence vs proof vs residual)
some narrative simply doesn't fit (cross-rule relation note doesn't fit falsifier or evidence)
ten_options_explored_full_ladder.cost: LOW grammar / MID semantic — no new keys, semantic reclassification is the work

framework_1.name: Parnas information-hiding via module-secret separation (1972)
framework_1.claim: modules should hide their secrets behind interfaces; strengthening history is a 'secret' that should not bleed into the rule's interface (60-line body)

framework_2.name: Brooks 'No Silver Bullet' essential-vs-accidental complexity (1986)
framework_2.claim: rule body cap is essential complexity (must not exceed cognitive grasp); strengthening history is accidental (grows without bound). Separate them.

framework_3.name: Putnam multi-realizability (1967, raw 106 reference)
framework_3.claim: the GENUS function 'note overflow handling' has multiple SPECIES realizations (annex / quad-root / structured-keys / append-only / dual-tier / structured-evidence). raw 106 mandates >=3 realizations — this cycle lists 10.

framework_4.name: Henderson append-only audit ledger (raw 77 lineage)
framework_4.claim: audit trails are append-only; mutation breaks reconstruction. note-as-mutable inside rule body violates this; .history append-only chain restores it.

counter_example_listed_raw_106.case: raw 1 os-lock — chflags uchg / chattr +i mechanism — note explains platform variance (Darwin vs Linux); EVENT-SPECIFIC platform note is invariant declaration, not strengthening accumulation. Separation NOT applicable.
counter_example_listed_raw_106.verdict: rule body 'note' justifiable when it describes invariant scope variance; cap of 2 is appropriate for invariants

counter_example_listed_raw_106.case: raw 7 / raw 8 file-taxonomy — historical rules with grandfathered notes from pre-2026-04-25 era; note is archaeological, not strengthening. Migration cost > benefit. Counter-example for the separation design.
counter_example_listed_raw_106.verdict: grandfathered rules retain inline note; new design applies only to NEW or POST-STRENGTHENED rules

counter_example_listed_raw_106.case: raw 102 raw-addition-request-disposition — note 2026-04-27 strengthening declares the workflow split (autonomous STRENGTHEN vs review-queue ADD-new). This IS the strengthening, not metadata about it. note = body content. Separation NOT applicable.
counter_example_listed_raw_106.verdict: rules whose body content IS a directive change (not history of the directive) keep inline note

tier_1_promotions.name: inline structured-keys (strengthening / history / evidence / incident)
tier_1_promotions.rationale: lowest cost (LOW), zero new files, zero raw 0 triad change required, raw 2 keys whitelist append only, fully passes 14-rule compatibility matrix. Matches raw 101 spec spirit (no redundant prose) by reclassifying overflow into structured-keys. Body line cap pressure remains but is the correct constraint to keep — forces thinking about minimality.
tier_1_promotions.paired_roadmap_id_raw_87: P-101.1 — keys-whitelist-extension {strengthening, history, evidence, incident, deferral}
implementation_steps: 1. raw 2 keys whitelist append: strengthening, history, evidence, incident, deferral, annex-ref
2. tool/ssot_minimal_lint.hexa: keep MAX_NOTES=2 cap on bare `note` only; new structured keys uncapped
3. retroactive convert raw 4 notes (5) → 1 note + 4 strengthening; raw 35 (4) → 1 note + 3 strengthening; raw 13/68/100/103 (3 each) → 1 note + 2 strengthening; raw 108 (4) → 1 note + 3 strengthening
4. self/raw_loader.hexa parser: add 5 keys to scope; preserve same-key-many-lines semantics
5. cross-repo via raw 47: sister repos pull updated raw 2 grammar pin
tier_1_promotions.cost_estimate: LOW — 4-6 hours total (1h grammar + 2h lint update + 2h retroactive conversion + 1h cross-repo trawl)
tier_1_promotions.omega_stop_satisfaction: fixpoint-convergence — adding more option exploration past option j yields 0 new design (10 options span the design space; fixpoint reached at iteration 1)

tier_1_promotions.name: dual-tier raw (CORE invariant + EXTENDED annex)
tier_1_promotions.rationale: complementary to T1 — solves the residual cases where structured-keys still don't fit (e.g., long incident narratives). EXTENDED tier file is opt-in per rule, glob-discoverable. Combines option a's per-rule-isolation with option c's grammar-stability.
implementation_steps: 1. raw 2 keys whitelist append: annex-ref (single value, points to .annex.<slug>)
2. .annex.<slug> file format = raw 2 grammar with single rule body (rule-id-mirror in header)
3. tool/ssot_minimal_lint.hexa: when annex-ref present, lint EXTENDED tier with relaxed cap (no body line cap, MAX_NOTES=8)
4. CORE tier remains 60 lines / 2 notes / 1500 chars / 8 follow-ups (raw 101 spec preserved)
5. cold-entry: tool/root_ssot_check.hexa scans .annex.* glob; .raw line points to annex-ref
tier_1_promotions.cost_estimate: MID — 8-12 hours total (3h grammar + 3h lint + 2h cold-entry update + 4h documentation + retroactive)
tier_1_promotions.omega_stop_satisfaction: categorical-limit — option i + option c jointly cover the complete design space; further options reduce to one of the two via decoupling

tier_1_promotions.name: .history append-only chain (raw 77 audit-ledger pattern)
tier_1_promotions.rationale: third tier for the strengthening-as-event-stream use case. raw 77 audit-ledger schema directly reusable. Best for incident records (kick-infra-fallback line 2391 evidence) and longitudinal trend audit. Less ergonomic for rule-local strengthening than T1/T2 but unmatched for cross-rule timeline analysis.
implementation_steps: 1. .history root file in each repo (raw 77 schema)
2. raw 0 STRENGTHEN: triad → quad (raw + own + guide + history) — contentious but justified by raw 77 pattern
3. raw 1 lock variant: uappnd (append-only) instead of uchg
5. cross-repo via raw 47: each sister repo bootstraps own .history
tier_1_promotions.cost_estimate: MID-HIGH — 12-16 hours (raw 0 STRENGTHEN governance + new root + lock variant + per-repo bootstrap)
tier_1_promotions.omega_stop_satisfaction: compute-saturation — beyond T1+T2+T3 the marginal utility per option drops below threshold; cycle terminates

scenario_A_raw_95_triad_mandate_breaks.trigger: T1 implementation makes structured-keys impossible to enforce at one of the three triad layers (e.g., os-level cannot distinguish note vs strengthening since both are byte streams)
scenario_A_raw_95_triad_mandate_breaks.fallback_choice: T2 option i — annex-tier shifts enforcement to the annex file (separate inode = os-level chflags trivially applies per-file)
scenario_A_raw_95_triad_mandate_breaks.rationale: annex isolation gives os-level natural file-boundary; structured-keys inside same file are hive-agent + cli-lint only (raw 95 still PASSES because rationale is in declared rule)

scenario_B_raw_2_grammar_extension_block.trigger: raw 2 keys whitelist freeze (e.g., self-format STRENGTHEN moratorium)
scenario_B_raw_2_grammar_extension_block.fallback_choice: T3 option d — .history is separate file with own grammar (raw 77 lineage) — does not require raw 2 keys extension

scenario_C_raw_0_triad_invariant_strict.trigger: user veto on triad → quad (raw 0 STRENGTHEN rejected)
scenario_C_raw_0_triad_invariant_strict.fallback_choice: T1 option c (no new root file) OR T2 option i (annex.* glob is .raw-derivative not new root)
scenario_C_raw_0_triad_invariant_strict.rationale: T3 (.history root quad) requires triad STRENGTHEN; if blocked, T1 / T2 are sufficient

scenario_D_all_T1_T2_T3_blocked.trigger: extreme governance constraint — no grammar change, no new files
scenario_D_all_T1_T2_T3_blocked.fallback_choice: option e (annex-as-attribute pointing to docs/<slug>.md, Tier-B markdown, raw 91 citation discipline only)
scenario_D_all_T1_T2_T3_blocked.rationale: weakest enforcement but zero SSOT change; explicit raw 91 citation discipline carries the audit burden

cost_estimate_per_option_summary.f_date_pinned_note_paragraphs: LOW grammar / HIGH governance — needs raw 101 STRENGTHEN

design_by_decoupling_raw_104_ladder.end_preserved: rule semantics + audit completeness + raw 0 triad invariant + raw 47 cross-repo portability
means_decoupled: SUBSCRIBE — sister-repo reuses parent .raw via raw-ref pin (existing)
PARTNER-SHARE — annex-tier glob shared across repos (T2)
MOCK-DETERMINISTIC — .history append-only with deterministic row hash (T3)
DRY-RUN-DEGRADED — option e markdown pointer (no enforcement, citation-only)
PROXY-BORROW — borrow raw 77 audit-ledger schema (T3 lineage)
design_by_decoupling_raw_104_ladder.ceiling_grade_raw_69: APPROACH — bounded improvement against raw 101 minimal-spec ceiling; no paradigm shift required

counter_examples: raw 1 os-lock platform note (invariant scope variance not strengthening)
raw 102 strengthening note (body content IS the directive change)

fixpoint_convergence.reason: 10 options enumerated; 11th option exploration yields no novel design surface — every option reduces to (location: same-file vs separate-file) × (grammar: extend vs reuse) × (mutation: append vs replace) = 8 cells; option j is the 8th cell; options g/f/h are pathological; no 11th cell exists

categorical_limit.reason: T1 (structured-keys) ∪ T2 (annex-tier) ∪ T3 (append-history) span the categorical product (in-file × per-file × cross-file). Other options are limit objects of these three.

compute_saturation.reason: 30-60min cycle budget consumed at this witness emit point; 30+ option exploration impossible without new tooling

mathematical_limit.reason: raw 101 minimal spec information-theoretic floor = body keys necessary to reconstruct rule semantics. Floor witnessed: status + slug + enforce + scope + why + proof + falsifier + 1-2 axes/keys = ~30 lines minimum. T1/T2/T3 all stay above floor.

common_patterns_found: all repos use raw 2 grammar (header + body keys, status whitelist)
all repos hit note-overflow on raws > 80 (deferred, blocker, design-strategy lineage)
no repo currently has annex / .history channel — universal gap
raw-ref pin propagation already solves L0 SSOT sync — extension cost is per-repo lint update only
raw_47_cross_repo_trawl_results.tier_1_promotion_universal_applicability: T1 (structured-keys) is universally applicable; T2 and T3 are opt-in per repo size (small repos with <50 rules don't need them)
